name: pr-followup-v2
description: Follows up on pull-request feedback, applies needed changes, verifies them, and reports whether the PR is ready.

coder.prompt_template:
Fetch the current state of PR {{job_payload.pr_url}}:
  gh pr view {{job_payload.pr_url}} --json number,title,url,state,isDraft,author,headRefName,headRefOid,baseRefName,mergeStateStatus,reviewDecision,latestReviews,comments,updatedAt,closed,mergedAt

Save the JSON to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-pr-state.json.

If the call returns 404 but the PR was previously known (job_payload may include a hint), return `visibility_blocked`.

STANCE: open | merged | closed_unmerged | not_found | visibility_blocked | failed

Fetch the checks for {{job_payload.pr_url}}:
  gh pr checks {{job_payload.pr_url}}

Save raw output to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-pr-checks.json (parse the table into a JSON array if helpful).

Classify:
- All checks passed → `checks_green`
- Any check failed → `checks_red`
- Some pending, none failed → `checks_pending`
- gh errored or no checks configured → `checks_unavailable`

Do NOT abort on `checks_unavailable` — continue the workflow.

STANCE: checks_green | checks_red | checks_pending | checks_unavailable

Determine what's new on {{job_payload.pr_url}} since the cutoff.

Cutoff source order:
1. job_payload.since_cutoff (if set)
2. submitted_pr.last_followup_at for this PR (operator-provided context in the payload)
3. submitted_pr.submitted_at as fallback

Use the state JSON from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-pr-state.json plus any additional gh calls needed (e.g. `gh pr view --json comments` again with timestamp filtering).

Produce /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-pr-delta.json with arrays: `new_comments`, `new_reviews`, `new_check_state_changes` (each entry with timestamp, actor, body or summary, source_url).

STANCE:
- `no_delta`: nothing new since cutoff → workflow ends at monitored_no_action
- `has_delta`: continue to classification
- `merged` / `closed`: state transition observed; route to terminal
- `visibility_blocked`: PR became inaccessible; escalate

Classify each entry in /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-pr-delta.json.

Deterministic pre-rules (apply first, don't waste budget on agent classification):
- actor matches `github-actions[bot]`, `dependabot[bot]`, `codecov[bot]`, similar → `bot_noise` (skip)
- body matches /^CLA|DCO|sign-off/i or actor is `cla-bot` → `cla_dco`
- review state `CHANGES_REQUESTED` → `request_changes`
- review state `APPROVED` + check_state `green` → `merge` (route may be approval → no_action with note)
- comment from PR author mentioning own commit → `bot_noise` (skip)

Agent classification for everything else, using this taxonomy:
- `maintainer_action`: maintainer asking for a code change, asking a question, requesting more info
- `maintainer_correction`: maintainer indicates the patch is wrong or the issue was misunderstood
- `ci_red`: CI failure trail explicitly tied to our changes
- `duplicate`: maintainer says "duplicate of #N" or "already fixed in #M"
- `security_sensitive`: anything involving credentials, supply chain, RCE — escalate
- `other`: ambiguous

Write /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-classifications.json.

Determine overall STANCE based on the highest-priority class observed:
- ci_red present → `ci_red`
- request_changes present → `request_changes`
- maintainer_correction present → `maintainer_correction`
- cla_dco present (and nothing more severe) → `cla_dco`
- duplicate/closed signal → `duplicate_or_closed`
- only bot_noise / no_action → `no_action`
- security_sensitive or `other`-ambiguous → `needs_human`

`cd {{project.local_path}}` first. classify_each_comment determined this PR is `ci_red` — investigate the failing checks on {{job_payload.pr_url}}.

1. Fetch the failing job log via `gh run view <run_id> --log` or `gh pr checks {{job_payload.pr_url}}` for the link.
2. Identify the failing job and failing command.
3. Determine relatedness to our patch:
   - Reproduce locally if practical (small command, no full-suite re-run)
   - If not practical, collect evidence (e.g. failure existed on main without our patch, intermittent failure history)
4. If we caused it AND fix is small: produce a candidate patch at /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-patch.diff (use this canonical name; downstream recommend_action expects it).
5. ALWAYS write findings to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md — at minimum a short note explaining why no action is needed (so the operator has SOMETHING to read at the Interactive gate).
6. Write detailed reasoning to /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-ci-investigation.md.

STANCE:
- `unrelated_or_flaky`: not our patch's fault → write a polite "CI failure looks unrelated to this PR; here's what I see" draft_response and no patch
- `related_patch_drafted`: small follow-up patch ready (patch.diff exists) + draft_response explaining the patch
- `related_needs_full_fix`: scope is too broad for inline patch; draft_response says we'll spawn a fix workflow; no patch
- `cannot_determine`: insufficient access or info; draft_response asks the maintainer for log access
- `needs_human`: anything risky

`cd {{project.local_path}}` first. classify_each_comment determined this PR has unresolved `request_changes` from a reviewer. Analyze the change requests from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-classifications.json (entries with class `request_changes`) and the full PR delta at /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-pr-delta.json.

For each request:
- If it's a clear code change → draft a follow-up patch.
- If it's a question → draft a textual response.
- If we believe the reviewer is mistaken → draft a response explaining (politely, with evidence).

**MANDATORY outputs (do NOT skip — recommend_action assumes these exist):**
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md — the public reply text (this is the artifact the operator reads at the Interactive gate)
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-review-analysis.md — your reasoning
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-patch.diff — patch if applicable (omit only if `comment_ready` STANCE)

Keep draft-response.md professional, concise, no emoji, no AI tells (claude/codex/gemini/anthropic/openai), no "as an AI" phrases.

STANCE:
- `patch_ready`: patch + response drafted (both files exist)
- `comment_ready`: response only, no code change (draft-response.md exists, patch.diff may be absent)
- `needs_clarification`: we need maintainer to clarify; draft-response.md ASKS for the clarification
- `should_withdraw`: reviewer is right; draft-response.md acknowledges + says we'll close
- `needs_human`: scope too unclear or risky

classify_each_comment determined a `maintainer_correction` on {{job_payload.pr_url}}. Analyze the correction entries in /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-classifications.json.

Rules:
- If the maintainer is right and our patch is wrong: draft an honest response acknowledging the error; recommend close/withdraw via STANCE: close_recommended.
- If the issue description was wrong but our patch fixes a valid variant: draft a clarification response.
- If the maintainer asks for evidence: produce concise reproduction/test evidence.
- If the correction implies a code change: STANCE needs_human (let operator decide if a re-classification to request_changes makes sense).

**MANDATORY outputs:**
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md — public reply text (always)
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-maintainer-correction-analysis.md — your reasoning
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-patch.diff — only if STANCE: patch_ready

Keep draft-response.md professional, concise, no emoji, no AI tells.

STANCE: comment_ready | patch_ready | close_recommended | wait | needs_human

classify_each_comment determined CLA/DCO signals on {{job_payload.pr_url}}. Analyze the bot comments from /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-classifications.json.

DO NOT forge legal action. CLA signing stays human action.

**MANDATORY outputs:**
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-paperwork-status.md — exact instructions + bot link + what the user must do
- /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md — short polite note explaining the paperwork state (operator may post this if helpful)

Outcomes:
- `user_action_required`: user must sign CLA or amend DCO; instructions in paperwork-status.md
- `empty_commit_retrigger_recommended`: bot expects a retrigger commit; draft the suggested `git commit --allow-empty -m '...'` command (DO NOT execute)
- `dco_patch_ready`: a DCO-style amend can be done locally (rebase + signoff); draft the steps as a patch under /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-patch.diff
- `wait`: bot still processing
- `needs_human`: ambiguous

STANCE: user_action_required | empty_commit_retrigger_recommended | dco_patch_ready | wait | needs_human

Reduce the prior branch's outcome into a single recommendation for {{job_payload.pr_url}}.

Classify outcome: `{{outcomes.classify_each_comment}}`. Only ONE of the branches below ran (deterministic routing from classify):
- ci: `{{outcomes.investigate_ci_red}}` (empty if classify was not ci_red)
- review: `{{outcomes.handle_reviewer_request_changes}}` (empty if classify was not request_changes)
- correction: `{{outcomes.handle_maintainer_correction}}` (empty if classify was not maintainer_correction)
- paperwork: `{{outcomes.handle_cla_dco}}` (empty if classify was not cla_dco)

**Verify the draft artifact exists before recommending comment_only / push_patch.** The active branch was REQUIRED to write /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md. Run `ls /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md` to confirm. If it's missing, STANCE: escalated (don't recommend comment_only without the artifact).

Write /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-recommendation.json.

STANCE map:
- recommended_action `wait` → STANCE: wait
- recommended_action `comment_only` → STANCE: comment_drafted (routes to operator_review_comment Interactive next)
- recommended_action `push_patch` → STANCE: patch_drafted (routes to operator_review_patch Interactive)
- recommended_action `close_pr` → STANCE: close_recommended → terminal closed_recommendation
- recommended_action `paperwork_user_action` → STANCE: paperwork_required → terminal paperwork_required
- recommended_action `escalate_human` → STANCE: escalated → terminal escalated_human

Phase 1: even when patch/comment is recommended, the workflow PARKS at the operator_review_* Interactive gate. NEVER execute the external action automatically.

agent.initial_prompt_template: PR follow-up: comment drafted for {{job_payload.pr_url}}. Review /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-draft-response.md and /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-recommendation.json. Choose: approve_comment (operator will run `gh pr comment` themselves), revise_comment, reject_comment, or wait. Signal done when decided.

coder.prompt_template:
The operator has reviewed the drafted comment for {{job_payload.pr_url}}. Based on the conversation context, determine their decision.

STANCE: approve_comment | revise_comment | reject_comment | wait

agent.initial_prompt_template: PR follow-up: patch drafted for {{job_payload.pr_url}}. Review /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-patch.diff and /tmp/lmctl-next-run-{{run_id}}-followup-recommendation.json. The patch is in {{project.local_path}}; HEAD is on the PR branch. Choose: approve_patch (operator will `git push` + optionally `gh pr comment` themselves), revise_patch, reject_patch, or wait. Signal done when decided.

coder.prompt_template:
The operator has reviewed the drafted patch for {{job_payload.pr_url}}. Based on the conversation context, determine their decision.

STANCE: approve_patch | revise_patch | reject_patch | wait
